free_text: Reaction cross sections on Ti, V, Ga and Ge targets
at E(p)=660 MeV.
Combined publication (in
Russian), see REL-REF
English Transl. of IZV,59,206
- cr.sect. on Ag-isotope
- cr.sect. on Zr isotopes
- cr.sect. on Sn isotopes
- cr.sect. on Mo isotopes
Irradiation time was 5-10 minutes.
First gamma-rays spectra were measured after
1.5-6 hours after irradiation. Each target was measured
25-30 times during to 1.5 -3 months.
Natural Ti, thickness 0.0430 g/cm2, was used.
Natural vanadium (V-51-99.75%, V-50-0.25%), thickness
0.0618 g/cm2, was used.
Natural Ga, thickness 0.471 g/cm2, was used.
Natural Ge, thickness 0.411 g/cm2, was used.
Experimental errors of the measurements,
which do not include errors of the gamma-yields,
half-lives and monitor reaction cross section.
The uncertainty in the calibration of
spectrometer is 4 %.
Tbl.1
(part of the same values are published in the
Bull.Acad.Sci.,59,895,1995)
Reference and Data Sections are corrected.
Small correction in reference.
Elem/Mass formalism is changed to simple
Reaction one.
Last checking has been done.
SD: REL-REF added. Author's names corrected
according to BAS publ. Part of info from METHOD moved
to SAMPLE.
